channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Security review planned for Pennsylvania governor's official residence after arson attack
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — An independent expert will review security at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence after investigators accused a man of scaling a wall and setting fire to the mansion, state police said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top Trump officials will hold talks with Europeans on the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Steve Witkoff, President Donald Trump’s special envoy, will travel to Paris this week for talks with European allies on U.S. efforts to negotiate a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Oklahoma City bombing was 30 years ago. Some survivors worry America didn’t learn the lesson
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Thirty years after a truck bomb detonated outside a federal building in America's heartland, killing 168 people in the deadliest homegrown attack on U.S. soil, deep scars remain.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AP Was There: A truck bomb rips through a federal building in Oklahoma City in 1995
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — EDITORS NOTE: On April 19, 1995, a former U.S. Army soldier parked a rented Ryder truck loaded with a powerful bomb made of fertilizer and fuel oil outside a federal office building in Oklahoma City. The blast at the Alfred P.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Starbucks introducing new dress code for baristas, a move protested by some
item.description: Baristas at your local Starbucks will soon be sporting a new, more consistent look after the company announced a new dress code this week.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration sues Maine over participation of transgender athletes in girls sports
item.description: The Trump administration is suing Maine’s education department for not complying with its push to ban transgender athletes in girls and women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Massachusetts man in gas mask pepper sprays officers in courthouse near Boston, video shows
item.description: Authorities in Massachusetts said Nicholas Akerberg, 28, attacked people inside a courthouse in a Boston suburb with pepper spray on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Ethiopian Orthodox Church in Washington preserves ancient traditions amid modern challenges
item.description: In the U.S. capital, hundreds of Ethiopians dressed in white tunics gather at the DSK Mariam Church to chant and pray in an ancient liturgical language of one of the oldest branches of Christianity.
item.dc:creator: Luis Andres Henao, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Wink Martindale, game show host and Elvis Presley interviewer, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the genial host of such hit game shows as “Gambit” and “Tic-Tac-Dough” who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: By BETH HARRIS Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's president accepts resignation of 2015 nuclear deal figure as UN atomic chief due in Tehran
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran 's president formally approved the resignation of one of his vice presidents who served as Tehran's key negotiator in its 2015 nuclear deal with world powers, just as the head of the United Nations' nuclear watchdog was due to arrive in the Islamic Republic on
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harvard stands to lose $2.2 billion in federal funding. Researchers fear science will suffer
item.description: CAMBRIDGE, Mass. (AP) — In a high-stakes standoff, President Donald Trump's administration says it will freeze $2.2 billion in federal research grants for Harvard University, which is pushing back on demands for changes to campus policy.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two Venezuelan illegal aliens charged with kidnapping, torturing, attempting to kill Washington state woman
item.description: ICE placed detainers on two illegal aliens from Venezuela in Washington who are accused of kidnapping, torturing, shooting and trying to kill a woman back in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man charged with arson at Pennsylvania governor's home struggled with mental health, brother says
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man accused of setting fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s mansion over the weekend had struggled with mental illness, twice being treated at a psychiatric hospital, as his life unraveled dramatically in the past few years, his brother said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video shows ICE agents shatter car window during Massachusetts arrest: attorney
item.description: Federal immigration agents are accused of shattering a car window in Massachusetts while detaining a Guatemalan man on Monday in a caught-on-camera incident – despite his attorney saying they had the wrong person.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, Fox 5

item.title: US judge presses Trump administration on its refusal to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge said Tuesday that she will order sworn testimony by Trump administration officials to determine if they complied with her orders to facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's a behind-the-scenes look of the 'Wicked' Broadway tour as it flies into Utah
item.description: As "Wicked" returns to 'defy gravity' for Utah audiences, ABC4 got a peek backstage to see how the show all comes together.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: NTSB finds 'failure' in training before deadly Newark cargo ship fire
item.description: The Newark fire department's lack of training and familiarity with marine firefighting during a deadly cargo ship fire at the East Coast's largest port in July 2023 amounted to a “failure of leadership,” the head of the National Transportation Safety Board said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actor William Levy arrested after drunken 'disturbance' at Florida restaurant: report
item.description: Cuban-American actor William Levy was arrested in South Florida Monday after causing a "disturbance" inside a restaurant, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rejects defense that Gaudreau brothers contributed to their deaths by cycling while impaired
item.description: SALEM, N.J. (AP) — The family of NHL hockey player Johnny Gaudreau and his brother Matthew listened in pain Tuesday as lawyers debated whether the men's own drinking contributed to their deaths when they were hit by an allegedly drunken and enraged driver as they cycled at night.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel: Liberals 'viciously attacking comedians' are a 'big part of why Trump' won
item.description: "I think a lot of the outrage is completely manufactured, and it’s like, a lot of these people who are angry aren’t really angry," Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: ICE blasts sanctuary county in Maryland after alleged murderer released from local custody
item.description: ICE arrested Rene Pop-Chub, 32, in Hyattsville, Maryland, on Saturday after authorities in Prince Georges County released him back into the community.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in federal funds for Harvard University
item.description: The Trump administration froze $2.2 billion worth of federal money for Harvard University after the school said it would not cave to the White House’s orders to limit activism or political speech on campus.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Shirley, KTLA 5

item.title: IRS tax deadline extended in multiple states: What to know
item.description: Residents in all or parts of 13 states can file for an extension to allow disaster-affected areas more time to submit their taxes.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, Fox 5

item.title: Obama-appointed judge shuts down Trump’s mass deportation plan and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: College football trophy comes apart in Vance's hands at White House
item.description: The College Football Playoff National Championship Trophy came apart during a White House visit by the Ohio State Buckeyes football team Monday as Vice President JD Vance tried to pick it up.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Salvadoran fugitive convicted in deadly attack of Maryland hiker
item.description: BEL AIR, Md. (AP) — A fugitive from El Salvador was convicted Monday in the 2023 slaying of a Maryland woman who was attacked while exercising on a popular hiking trail northeast of Baltimore.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, KRON4

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KTLA 5

item.title: Massive bluefin tuna catches halted at pier after officials step in, manager says
item.description: For several days a huge bluefin tuna were caught at Jennette's Pier in Nags Head, but the bluefin fun came to an end after an official visited last week, the pier said.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reported intruder arrested near UnitedHealthcare headquarters months after CEO's killing
item.description: Police in the Minneapolis suburb of Minnetonka said the suspect was arrested outside of the United Healthcare corporate campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump wants Congress to end daylight saving time
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday urged Congress to “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in his latest dig at the semiannual changing of clocks.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A celestial 'smiley face' will be visible to stargazers later this month: What to know
item.description: Stargazers who turn their eyes skyward — and use a little bit of imagination — will have a chance to spot a rare "smiley face" in space later this month.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, ABC 4
